title: Trump's tariffs, Fed minutes, Rumble's Bitcoin buy - what's moving markets
United States Dollar Index holds above 101.00 following US strikes on Iran
summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is holding gains for the second consecutive day and trading around 101.10 during the Asian session on Wednesday.

title: India Gold price today: Gold rises, according to FXStreet data
summary: Gold prices rose in India on Wednesday, according to data compiled by FXStreet.

title: Silver climbs above $60.00 despite US–Iran tensions, Fed minutes in focus
summary: Silver price (XAG/USD) attracts some buyers to near $60.35, snapping the two-day losing streak during the early European session on Wednesday. The white metal edges higher amid a softer US Dollar (USD) ahead of the release of the June FOMC meeting Minutes.

title: Gold: Central bank demand underpins prices – ING
summary: ING strategists Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey report that Gold has edged lower after an early advance as traders await the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes, but the metal trades in line with evolving US rate expectations and remains supported above $4,000/oz.

title: Oil: Gradual downside with security premium – OCBC
summary: OCBC Bank’s Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that Oil and tech-led equity weakness have driven a stronger US Dollar, higher global yields and softer Gold.

title: Gold bulls seem hesitant as Iran tensions fuel inflation fears and hawkish Fed bets
summary: Gold (XAU/USD) maintains its bid tone heading into the European session on Wednesday and, for now, seems to have snapped a two-day losing streak to sub-$4,100 levels, or the weekly trough touched the previous day.

title: Czech Koruna: CNB hawkish bias supports CZK – Commerzbank
summary: Commerzbank’s Tatha Ghose notes Czech headline inflation fell to 1.5% year-on-year, below Czech National Bank (CNB) forecasts, with goods and energy prices soft but services still elevated.

title: Europe stocks fall as geopolitical flusion and Fed anxiousness weigh
